definitions.text: A sequence of states representing an encoded message in a communication channel.
Any variation of a quantity or change in an entity over time that conveys information upon detection.
A sign made to give notice of some occurrence, command, or danger, or to indicate the start of a concerted action.
To indicate; to convey or communicate by a signal.
examples: I signalled my acquiescence with a nod.
He whistled to signal that we should stop.

definitions.text: To communicate with (a person or system) by a signal.
examples: Seeing the flames, he ran to the control room and signalled headquarters.

definitions.text: Standing above others in rank, importance, or achievement.
examples: a signal exploit; a signal success; a signal act of benevolence
